data.title: PINE: AFFO per share up 32% year-over-year; dividend raised 6.7% amid strong investment activity
data.description:
AFFO and FFO per share grew over 30% year-over-year, driven by robust investment activity and a strong commercial loan portfolio. Dividend was raised 6.7%, and guidance for FFO and AFFO was increased, with a focus on high-quality, investment-grade tenants and a strong acquisition pipeline.

This is an AI-generated summary and may contain inaccuracies. Please verify any important information with the original source.

NOTES ON FORM 8.3

1.             See the definition of "connected fund manager" in Rule 2.2 of Part A of the Rules.

2.             See the definition of "interest in a relevant security" in Rule 2.5 of Part A of the Rules and see Rule 8.6(a) and (b) of Part B of the Rules.

3.             See the definition of "relevant securities" in Rule 2.1 of Part A of the Rules.

4.             See the definition of "dealing" in Rule 2.1 of Part A of the Rules.

5.             If the economic exposure to changes in the price of securities is limited, for example, by virtue of a stop loss arrangement relating to a spread bet, full details must be given.

6.             See Rule 2.5(d) of Part A of the Rules.

7.             If details included in a disclosure under Rule 8 are incorrect, they should be corrected as soon as practicable in a subsequent disclosure. Such disclosure should state clearly that it corrects details disclosed previously, identify the disclosure or disclosures being corrected, and provide sufficient detail for the reader to understand the nature of the corrections. In the case of any doubt, the Panel should be consulted.

For full details of disclosure requirements, see Rule 8 of the Rules. If in doubt, consult the Panel.

References in these notes to "the Rules" are to the Irish Takeover Panel Act, 1997, Takeover Rules, 2022.

data.title: AMTB: Net income and deposits rose, driven by international growth and disciplined credit actions
data.description:
Second quarter 2026 saw higher net income, strong deposit growth—especially from Venezuela—and improved capital ratios. Loan growth focused on C&I and residential mortgages, while efficiency initiatives and disciplined credit management supported profitability.

This is an AI-generated summary and may contain inaccuracies. Please verify any important information with the original source.

data.title: Six Flags Entertainment Is Maintained at Buy by Guggenheim
KNSL: EPS up 15.9%, ROE 24.4%, strong underwriting, expanded $337M buyback, growth outside property
data.description:
Operating EPS rose 15.9% and ROE reached 24.4%, despite a 5% drop in gross written premium. Excluding Commercial Property, premium growth was positive, and strong underwriting and investment results drove profitability. Buyback authorization increased to $337 million.

Based on

Disclaimer
This is an AI-generated summary and may contain inaccuracies. Please verify any important information with the original source.

data.title: GREENPLY: Q1 FY 2027 delivered robust revenue and volume growth, with margins and expansion on track
data.description:
Q1 FY 2027 saw 20.7% revenue growth and double-digit volume gains in both plywood and MDF, with margins expanding and new capacity coming online. CapEx and debt remain within guidance, and the company targets strong volume and margin growth for the year.

Based on

Disclaimer
This is an AI-generated summary and may contain inaccuracies. Please verify any important information with the original source.

data.title: FLG: Third consecutive profitable quarter with record C&I growth, lower CRE, and a $250M buyback
data.description:
Profitability improved for the third straight quarter, driven by record C&I loan growth, disciplined expense management, and a strong capital position. CRE exposure and criticized loans declined, while a $250 million share buyback was announced. EPS guidance was revised lower due to higher CRE payoffs and deposit mix shifts.

Based on

Disclaimer
This is an AI-generated summary and may contain inaccuracies. Please verify any important information with the original source.

data.title: ABCB: Strong core profitability, robust growth, and stable credit quality drive positive results
data.description:
Core profitability remained strong with robust loan and deposit growth, stable asset quality, and a solid capital position. Adjusted net income was $107.3 million, and the company expanded into Nashville to support long-term growth. Margin compression is expected due to deposit cost pressures.

Disclaimer
This is an AI-generated summary and may contain inaccuracies. Please verify any important information with the original source.

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Stuck In Range — Market Talk
SIGI: Strong investment income and disciplined underwriting drove double-digit operating ROE
data.description:
Delivered strong investment income and double-digit operating ROE, with all segments profitable despite premium declines and competitive pressures. Guidance for combined ratio and investment income was reaffirmed or raised, while capital management remains disciplined and focused on long-term growth.

VCP: Q1 FY 2027 delivered strong growth and profitability, with refinancing reducing debt and supporting recovery
data.description:
Revenue and EBITDA declined in FY 2026 due to lower volumes and macro headwinds, but Q1 FY 2027 showed strong recovery with market share gains and improved profitability. Major refinancing reduced debt and extended maturities, while operational improvements and asset sales support a positive outlook for FY 2027 and beyond.
